title: Benchmark angles
topic_path: Module 4: Angle measure and plane figures

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these angles has a measure of $270^\circ?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can identify a $180^\circ$ angle.  It looks like a straight line.  



[[☃ image 1]]

The $270^\circ$ angle will have to be larger than that one.

This angle is a $270^\circ$ angle.  


[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![An angle that shows a little more than a half turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4e9a8646ff5d8fb4748636bf4bb7c4edb677934a)
![An angle that shows a little less than an eighth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/93662f99aeca40d3c9b55989d5bb09ae3098bc50)
![An angle that shows exactly a half turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9669c8ff7732ed7df413a36119f249576a38fb47)
![An angle that shows about a three-fourths turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/acd87469de5e31d8bf43f19ee5305713e8b71ea5)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these angles has a measure of $90^\circ?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can identify a $90^\circ$ angle.  It looks like the corner of a rectangular piece of paper or a door.  

[[☃ image 1]]

A $90^\circ$ angle can be turned in any direction.

This angle is a $90^\circ$ angle.  

[[☃ image 1]]

![An angle that shows exactly a one-fourth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/210ce45cd0b0fab8c44830252ae050b343319332)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which image shows a marked angle that measures $90^\circ?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A $90^\circ$ angle looks like the corner of a rectangular piece of paper or a door.  Sometimes, it is marked with a little square inside of the angle.

[[☃ image 1]]

This is the shape with a marked angle that measures $90^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A three-sided shape with an arrow pointing to the angle made by the left corner that is about a one-sixth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c0371fc888f34784f5fb002952e7c426d4bfd9a1)

![A five-sided shape with an arrow pointing to the angle made by the bottom left corner that is a little more than a one-fourth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb11310b4364a24360378fe7931d923d471a948b)

![A four-sided shape with an arrow pointing to the angle made by the upper left corner that is about a  one-fourth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5a2203538a80015f4cfb65e5c1648bf2effeb65a)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is closest to the measure of the angle?**

[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can most easily identify a $90^\circ$ angle.  It looks like the corner of a rectangular piece of paper or a door.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Does the angle in this problem look square like a $90^\circ$ angle?

The angle is smaller than a $90^\circ$ angle.
The only answer choice that is smaller than $90^\circ$ is $60^\circ$.
The measure of the angle is $60^\circ$.

options.alt: An angle that shows about a sixth of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is closest to the measure of the angle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can most easily identify a $90^\circ$ angle.  It looks like the corner of a rectangular piece of paper or a door.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Does the angle in this problem look square like a $90^\circ$ angle?
We often see a square marking on $90^\circ$ angles to indicate that they are right angles.  When we see this, we can assume that the measure of the angle is $90^\circ$.
The measure of the angle is $90^\circ$.

options.alt: An angle that shows exactly one-fourth of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is closest to the measure of the angle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can most easily identify a $90^\circ$ angle.  It looks like the corner of a rectangular piece of paper or a door.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Does the angle in this problem look square like a $90^\circ$ angle?

The angle looks smaller than a $90^\circ$ angle.
The only answer choice that is smaller than $90^\circ$ is $30^\circ$.
The measure of the angle is $30^\circ$.

options.alt: An angle that shows a little less than an eighth of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is closest to the measure of the angle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can most easily identify a $90^\circ$ angle.  It looks like the corner of a rectangular piece of paper or a door.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Does the angle in this problem look square like a $90^\circ$ angle?

The angle looks larger than $90^\circ$.

A $180^\circ$ angle is twice the size of a $90^\circ$ angle.  It is called a *straight angle* and happens when one side of the angle rotates around to point in the opposite direction of the other side.  It looks like this:  

[[☃ image 1]]
The angle looks bigger than a $90^\circ$ angle and smaller than a $180^\circ$ angle.  What angle could that be?
The measure of the angle is $135^\circ$.

options.alt: An angle that shows a little more than a third of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is closest to the measure of the angle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can most easily identify a $90^\circ$ angle.  It looks like the corner of a rectangular piece of paper or a door.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Does the angle in this problem look square like a $90^\circ$ angle?

A $180^\circ$ angle is twice the size of a $90^\circ$ angle.  It is called a *straight angle* and happens when one side of the angle rotates around to point in the opposite direction of the other side.  It looks like this:  

[[☃ image 1]]
The measure of the angle is $180^\circ$.

options.alt: An angle that shows exactly a half of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is closest to the measure of the marked angle in the shape?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can most easily identify a $90^\circ$ angle.  It looks like the corner of a rectangular piece of paper or a door.  


[[☃ image 1]]

Does the angle in this problem look square like a $90^\circ$ angle?

The angle is a little smaller than a $90^\circ$ angle.


Which choice is a little smaller than $90^\circ$?
The measure of the angle is $60^\circ$.

options.alt: A four-sided shape with an arrow pointing to the angle made by the bottom left corner that is larger than one-eighth of a turn but smaller than one-fourth of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is closest to the measure of the marked angle in the shape?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can most easily identify a $90^\circ$ angle.  It looks like the corner of a rectangular piece of paper or a door.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Does the angle in this problem look square like a $90^\circ$ angle?

The angle looks larger than $90^\circ$.

A $180^\circ$ angle is twice the size of a $90^\circ$ angle.  It is called a *straight angle* and happens when one side of the angle rotates around to point in the opposite direction of the other side.  It looks like this:  

[[☃ image 1]]
The angle looks bigger than a $90^\circ$ angle and smaller than a $180^\circ$ angle.  What angle could that be?
The measure of the angle is about $135^\circ$.

options.alt: A four-sided shape with an arrow pointing to the angle made by the bottom left corner that is about a one-third of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match these angles with their measures.**

[[☃ matcher 2]]
hints: Let's arrange the angles from smallest to largest.
The smallest angle is $30^\circ$, next is $60^\circ$, next is $90^\circ$, and the largest is $180^\circ$.

$30^\circ$ | $60^\circ$ | $90^\circ$ | $180^\circ$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
![An angle that shows a little less than an eighth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a99dbac62d0536285b27997a77e6aec97952698e) | ![An angle that shows about a sixth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c83ac2a6a822d460a8a567430ad434c61fb025e3) | ![An angle that shows exactly a one-fourth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1a1469c3bc93433b311d7d18c8e53e49c1841890) | ![An angle that shows exactly a half turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b47aa77b2939cdf739fea9f11f2048c2aaba38d)

Now, let's match them in the table.

Measure | Angle
- | - | -
$30^\circ$ | ![An angle that shows a little less than an eighth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a99dbac62d0536285b27997a77e6aec97952698e)
$180^\circ$ | ![An angle that shows exactly a half turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b47aa77b2939cdf739fea9f11f2048c2aaba38d)
$60^\circ$ |  ![An angle that shows about a sixth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c83ac2a6a822d460a8a567430ad434c61fb025e3) 
$90^\circ$ | ![An angle that shows exactly a one-fourth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1a1469c3bc93433b311d7d18c8e53e49c1841890)

right: ![An angle that shows a little less than an eighth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a99dbac62d0536285b27997a77e6aec97952698e)
![An angle that shows exactly a half turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b47aa77b2939cdf739fea9f11f2048c2aaba38d)
![An angle that shows about a sixth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c83ac2a6a822d460a8a567430ad434c61fb025e3)
![An angle that shows exactly a one-fourth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1a1469c3bc93433b311d7d18c8e53e49c1841890)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match these angles with their measures.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: Let's arrange the angles from smallest to largest.
The smallest angle is $30^\circ$, next is $60^\circ$, next is $90^\circ$, and the largest is $135^\circ$.

$30^\circ$ | $60^\circ$ | $90^\circ$ | $135^\circ$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
![An angle that shows a little less than an eighth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a21f4e2266127b78c45c1aa10b188320d1d92cb2) | ![An angle that shows about a sixth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/efa0c941f4b7afdedd02d4bcedf95b40c0799158) | ![An angle that shows exactly a one-fourth of a turn.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/241dc1c1267fb84864c5bc349c6077530ca9b13e) | ![An angle that shows a little more than a third of a turn.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bbdd6c920f61689de7e75b02c423b1f8.png)

Now, let's match them in the table.

Measure | Angle
- | - | -
